articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israeli Airstrikes Shake Beirut and Drone Attacks Hit Tehran
articles.content: The fighting in the Middle East showed no sign of letup, as the Pentagon told Congress that the first six days of the war had cost the U.S. at least $11.3 billion.

articles.title: How Trump’s War With Iran Changed the World in a Week
articles.content: The conflict is reshaping travel patterns, energy dependencies, living costs, trade routes and diplomatic alliances.

articles.title: Starmer Was Warned About Risk of Appointing Friend of Epstein As Ambassador
articles.content: British officials told Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Peter Mandelson’s close ties to Jeffrey Epstein before Mr. Mandelson was appointed U.S. ambassador.

articles.title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
articles.content: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.

articles.title: Why Did the UK Police Repeatedly Decline to Investigate Claims About Epstein and Prince Andrew?
articles.content: The police in London interviewed Virginia Giuffre three times over her allegations about Jeffrey Epstein, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Ghislaine Maxwell, but never began a criminal investigation.

articles.title: U.S. Seeks Extradition of Maduro Ally Alex Saab From Venezuela
articles.content: The indicted tycoon Alex Saab is being detained in Venezuela on a U.S. request. His case tests President Trump’s sway over the country’s new rulers.

articles.title: A Trump-Xi Summit Nears, but China Doesn’t Know What Trump Wants
articles.content: Trump’s agenda for the high-stakes meeting remains unclear to Beijing, Chinese analysts say, while American executives say they haven’t been invited along.

articles.title: China’s Military Has Quietly Cut Flying Near Taiwan
articles.content: For years, China has flown military jets near Taiwan almost daily. Then they suddenly stopped, leaving analysts to wonder why.

articles.title: Eight Arab, Islamic countries condemn Israeli closure of Al-Aqsa Mosque
articles.content: For the past 12 days, Israel has closed Al-Aqsa Mosque and restricted movement in the Old City of Jerusalem.

articles.title: Iran war live: Oil tankers hit in Iraq, Tehran sets 3 conditions for peace
articles.content: Iran demands recognition of its rights, war reparations and guarantees against future aggression to end the war.

articles.title: ‘Proud as a Qatari’: Country’s PM salutes resilience amid Iranian attacks
articles.content: The country's prime minister urges resilience, says government committed to ensuring people's lives are not disrupted.

articles.title: Massive fires on two oil tankers after attack in Iraqi waters
articles.content: Two foreign tankers were seen ablaze in Iraqi territorial waters after a strike near the al-Faw port.

articles.title: New files show British PM warned of ‘reputational risks’ with Mandelson
articles.content: More than 100 pages of documents were published which covered the process of Peter Mandelson’s appointment as US envoy.

articles.title: Trump: ‘I don’t know’ about report US at fault in school strike
articles.content: Donald Trump denied knowledge of a military investigation finding the US responsible for the Minab girls' school strike.

articles.title: South Africa summons new US ambassador over ‘undiplomatic remarks’
articles.content: US President Trump has claimed South Africa is engaged in a campaign of white persecution, which Pretoria denies.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers hit medical giant Stryker in retaliatory cyberattack
articles.content: Group claims to have seized 50 terabytes of data in response to deadly US-Israeli strikes on Iranian school.

articles.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
articles.content: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

articles.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
articles.content: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

articles.title: China's biggest political meeting is ending - what have we learned?
articles.content: BBC correspondents give us their biggest takeaways from China's National People's Congress.

articles.title: Countries agree to record release of emergency oil reserves as prices surge
articles.content: The G7 group of nations welcomes the idea of releasing oil in response to the surge in prices since the US-Israel war with Iran began

articles.title: 'Even under missiles we carry on living' - how young Iranians are coping with war
articles.content: Iranians say they are sheltering at home and rarely venturing out on near-empty streets as the US-Israeli bombing campaign continues.

articles.title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
articles.content: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.

articles.title: Kanye West ordered to pay $140K in Malibu mansion renovation lawsuit
articles.content: A handyman had sought $1.7m (£1.267m) from the rapper over claims of unpaid work, medical expenses and being unfairly fired.

articles.title: Three brothers arrested after explosion at US embassy in Oslo
articles.content: Norway's police attorney says the authorities are investigating whether a foreign state actor was involved.

articles.title: FBI warns Iran aspired to attack California with drones in retaliation for war: Alert
articles.content: The warning came in a bulletin that was reviewed by ABC News.

articles.title: Dems call for probe into 'troubling pattern' of legal wins for Pam Bondi's brother
articles.content: Two Democrats are calling on the DOJ internal watchdog to launch a probe into what they say is a "troubling pattern" of favorable outcomes for Brad Bondi's clients.

articles.title: 12-year-old girl dies days after collapsing following fight near school bus stop
articles.content: Police are investigating the death of a 12-year-old girl who was hospitalized after a fistfight near a school bus stop in her neighborhood

articles.title: WATCH:  Woman with cancer has ‘no evidence of disease’ after life-changing transplant
articles.content: Amy Piccioli was diagnosed with stage 4 colorectal cancer at the age of 39.

articles.title: WATCH:  Whale of a story in Hawaii
articles.content: Witnessing a breaching whale is a thrill, but whale watchers in Maui got a rare treat of seeing three whales breaching at once.

articles.title: WATCH:  Jennifer Lopez pulls back the curtain of Las Vegas residency
articles.content: The star opens up about her new Las Vegas residency, life with her family and why she says she's entering what she calls her "happy era."

articles.title: WATCH:  Miami Heat’s Bam Adebayo scores historic 83-point game
articles.content: Adebayo scored 83 points against the Washington Wizards, the second most ever in a game, only behind Wilt Chamberlain’s famous 100-point scoring game and overtaking Kobe Bryant’s record.

articles.title: Senegal passes law doubling penalty for same-sex relations to 10 years in prison
articles.content: Senegal’s parliament on Wednesday passed a law doubling the maximum prison term for same-sex relations to 10 years, intensifying a crackdown on the LGBTQ community. The bill, which also criminalizes promoting or financing such relationships, now awaits President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s signature amid reports of dozens of arrests since February.

articles.title: Banking, finance and consultancy firms evacuate staff in Gulf States
articles.content: Several major international businesses closed or evacuated offices in Gulf countries after Iran threatened US- and Israel-linked economic targets and centres in the Middle East. US finance group Citi and British consultancy Deloitte evacuated offices in Dubai's financial centre, while PwC, another British consultancy, closed offices in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE and Kuwait. HSBC has closed all branches in Qatar until further notice.

articles.title: PSG stun Chelsea as Jorgensen blunder sparks Champions League thriller
articles.content: Paris St Germain beat Chelsea 5-2 in a thrilling Champions League last-16 first leg on Wednesday, helped by a costly error from goalkeeper Filip Jorgensen and two late goals from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia. The sides had been level at 2-2 after Chelsea twice came from behind at the Parc des Princes.

articles.title: 'Two decades in the making': expert details how Mossad built its network of spies in Iran
articles.content: Iran has ⁠arrested dozens including ​a foreign national for allegedly spying for the country's "enemies". Dr Aviva Guttmann, Lecturer in Strategy and Intelligence at the Department of International Politics at Aberystwyth University, details how Mossad has inflirtated Iran's security apparatus over the past two decades. She also explains that Iranian intelligence is resorting to using "disposable agents" for its operations in Israel.

articles.title: US war on Iran: Using military force to achieve political outcomes rarely produces lasting stability
articles.content: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dafydd Townley, Teaching Fellow in US politics and International Security at the University of Portsmouth. For Mr. Townley, the most striking feature of the current US operation against Iran is the gap between clear military action and ambiguous political strategy. The Pentagon has articulated core military objectives, which includes weakening the country’s military. What remains far less clear, however, is the political end game that these operations are meant to achieve.

articles.title: Israel launches new 'large-scale' wave of strikes on south Beirut
articles.content: The Israeli military said Wednesday that it had launched a new wave of strikes on Hezbollah targets in the southern Beirut suburbs, vowing to act with "great force" in the area. The military said it had begun a "large-scale wave of strikes" on Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahiyeh, the Lebanese capital's southern suburb, where the Iran-backed group holds sway. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent reports from Beirut.

articles.title: Drone strike hits Goma killing aid worker and civilians
articles.content: In tonight's edition, a French aid worker is killed by a drone strike in Goma in eastern DRC. Also,  global cocoa prices plunged after 2024's record highs, forcing producers to cut payments to farmers. And after nearly 170 years, leopards are returning to South Africa’s western coast, a real conservation success.

articles.title: Iran may tap into organised crime to carry out terror attacks, expert says
articles.content: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Graig Klein, Assistant Professor in Terrorism and Political Violence at Leiden University in The Hague, explains that Iran may tap into organised crime in order to carry out terror attacks in Europe in retaliation for the war waged by the US and Israel, adding that Donald Trump is being complacent about the threat as the latest incidents targeting US embassies abroad "is part of the Iranian strategic playbook".

articles.title: Iran war: Tehran demands reparations to end conflict
articles.content: Iran's president has set conditions for an end to the war, including reparations and guarantees against future aggression. Meanwhile, the IEA's 32 member states agreed to release 400 million barrels of oil. Follow live.

articles.title: Spain withdraws ambassador to Israel as rift intensifies
articles.content: Spain's government has formally dismissed its ambassador to Israel, six months after recalling her in a dispute prompted by the war in Gaza. Its Tel Aviv embassy will be led by a charge d'affaires until further notice.

articles.title: Iran signals 2026 World Cup boycott after US strikes
articles.content: Iran's possible withdrawal would test FIFA's ability to keep the World Cup insulated from geopolitics. With Iran signaling it may boycott the event, the field for the sport's biggest tournament now looks less settled.

articles.title: UK government releases Mandelson US appointment files
articles.content: Keir Starmer's government has released internal documents on the appointment of known Jeffrey Epstein associate Peter Mandelson as US ambassador in 2024. The prime minsiter was warned of a "general reputational risk."

articles.title: How Poland is flexing its economic muscle in Western Europe
articles.content: Poland's economy is now a European star performer. A new trend suggests further momentum, with more and more Polish firms acquiring foreign companies, including in Germany.

articles.title: Has Cambodia really cut cybercrime by half?
articles.content: Cambodia claims to be finally dismantling the cyber-scam compounds that have made the country a global fraud hub, but analysts warn there is little proof that top figures in the scam industry will face consequences.

articles.title: Trump's arch plan and the history of gateways
articles.content: The proposed 250‑foot arch has sparked debate in Washington. But monumental gateways have shaped politics, memory, and ritual across cultures for millennia.

articles.title: Rohingya risk lives at sea to flee Myanmar, Bangladesh
articles.content: Every year, thousands of Rohingya attempt to flee Myanmar and Bangladesh by sea. The escape effort kills hundreds of people per year.

articles.title: Social media firms asked to toughen up age checks for under-13s
articles.content: Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube and Roblox are among the platforms UK regulators say aren't putting children's safety at the heart of their products.

articles.title: What role has cyber warfare played in Iran?
articles.content: Militaries are often cagey about their cyber activities. But the US has hinted at the role it has played.

articles.title: US launches probe into major trading partners after tariffs struck down
articles.content: The move comes after the US Supreme Court struck down a key part of Donald Trump's tariffs policies last month.

articles.title: Big Tech backs Anthropic in fight against Trump administration
articles.content: A group representing tech giants called government action against Anthropic a "temper tantrum".

articles.title: A small US grocer is calling out the lower prices at big chains
articles.content: It is 'impossible for us to compete', says the boss of a New York grocery store.

articles.title: Mortgage rates rise and deals pulled over Iran war turmoil
articles.content: Average mortgage rates hit highest since last August in the biggest upheaval since the mini-Budget.

articles.title: Fuel tax hike plan to be kept under review over Iran, says PM
articles.content: Fuel duty on petrol and diesel is due to rise from September, when a 5p cut is phased out.

articles.title: At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan
articles.content:
Strike in Shukeiri killed schoolgirls, teachers and healthcare workers in latest incident in three-year war

At least 17 people, most of them schoolgirls, were killed on Wednesday when an explosive-laden drone blamed on Sudan’s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces struck a secondary school and a health care centre.

At least 10 people were wounded in the strike in the village of Shukeiri in the White Nile province, according to Dr Musa al-Majeri, director of Douiem hospital, the nearest major medical facility to the village.

Continue reading...

articles.title: French aid worker among three killed in dronestrike in east DRC, M23 rebels say
articles.content:
Rebel group blames government for attack on residential area of M23-controlled city of Goma

Three people including a French UN aid worker have been killed in a drone attack in Goma, a spokesperson for the M23 rebel group has said.

The attack took place at about 4am on Wednesday in the upmarket residential neighbourhood of Himbi in the city, which has been under M23 occupation since January 2025.

Continue reading...

articles.title: At least 65 Nigerian soldiers killed in jihadist raids in country’s north-east
articles.content:
Gunmen from Islamic State West Africa Province overran four military bases and abducted 300 civilians, say reports

At least 65 Nigerian soldiers have been killed in jihadist raids across the country’s north-east in the last two weeks, as the west African state battles to contain one of the world’s deadliest terror groups.

On 5 and 6 March, gunmen from Islamic State West Africa Province (Iswap) overran four military bases in Borno state, the epicentre of the insurgency. Nigerian daily the Punch reported that about 40 soldiers were killed in total in these attacks.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Experts fear ‘unethical’ vaccine trial in Africa is ‘prototype’ for US studies under RFK Jr
articles.content:
Danish researchers whose work on effects of vaccines has been called into question are at center of US vaccine policy

New details are leading experts to fear that an “unethical” vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau is the “prototype” for studies under Robert F Kennedy Jr, secretary of the US department of health and human services (HHS) and longtime vaccine critic.

At the center of US vaccine policy is an unlikely set of Danish researchers whose work on the health effects of vaccines has been called into question. The study in Guinea-Bissau would have looked at the overall health effects of giving hepatitis B vaccines by only vaccinating half of the newborns in the study at birth despite an 18% prevalence rate in adults of the illness, which can lead to serious and sometimes fatal health consequences.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Prison sentences for pair who attacked gay men hailed as sign of hope for Kenya’s LGBTQ+ community
articles.content:
The perpetrators were jailed for 15 years for robbery with violence in the east African country, where homophobic attacks are increasing

The sentencing of two people who attacked and robbed two gay men in Kenya has been hailed by LGBTQ+ rights advocates as a breakthrough and a sign of hope for the country’s queer community. “Abel Meli & Another” were sentenced to 15 years in prison for robbery with violence on 3 March at Milimani law courts in Nairobi.

The ruling is a rare example of justice being served for the queer community in Kenya. Njeri Gateru, the executive director of the National Gay and Lesbian Human Rights Commission, an independent human rights institution working towards equality for sexual and gender minorities in Kenya, said: “A lot is going against [the queer community] with the existence of the criminal laws and prevailing homophobic attitudes, but some of us still trust that we can find justice, so this case encourages us.”

Continue reading...

articles.title: El Salvador’s mass arrest policy may have led to crimes against humanity, study shows
articles.content:
Experts documented murder, torture and disappearances under Nayib Bukele’s policy targeting gangs

The draconian mass incarceration policy of El Salvador’s president, Nayib Bukele, may have led to crimes against humanity, according to a new study by legal experts.

By locking up 1.4% of the population without due process, Bukele turned El Salvador from one of Latin America’s most violent countries into one of its least violent – but at the cost of human rights and the rule of law.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Argentina grants asylum to Brasília rioter in move that may sway Brazil vote
articles.content:
Decision to shield pro-Bolsonaro truck driver sentenced for 8 January 2023 attack could inflame Brazil election politics

Argentina has granted asylum to a Brazilian fugitive convicted for his role in 2023 pro-Bolsonaro riots – a decision that analysts say could reverberate in Brazil’s upcoming presidential election.

A week after Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Brazil’s president, took office, hundreds of people ransacked Brazil’s congress building, presidential palace and supreme court on 8 January 2023, in an attempt to overturn former president Jair Bolsonaro’s electoral defeat. Investigators later concluded the attacks were the culmination of a broader plot aimed at staging a coup.

Continue reading...

articles.title: José Antonio Kast, the Pinochet fan about to swerve Chile to the far right
articles.content:
The new president won office by promising to clean up crime, but his background is red rag to a bull for many

Just south of Santiago, the tiny rural town of Paine is a quiet grid of painted adobe facades, shaded squares and shuttered shop fronts as the summer holidays draw to a close.

But the white-knuckle fear of crime that propelled its most famous son, José Antonio Kast, to a resounding victory in December’s presidential election is as present in sleepy Paine as it is the length of Chile.

Continue reading...

articles.title: Stocks Slide, Oil Advances as Iran War Extends: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity futures and Asian stocks declined on Thursday, extending a volatile week, as another rally in oil prices and mounting strain in the private credit market weighed on investor sentiment.

articles.title: Oil Advances as Middle East Disruptions Outweigh Release Plan
articles.content: Oil climbed for a second day after another volatile session as traders focused on widespread disruptions due to the Iran war, with a record release of emergency reserves doing little to calm the market.

articles.title: Soybeans in Spotlight as Trade Chiefs Prepare for Trump-Xi Meet
articles.content: Soybeans are likely to be on the agenda when trade chiefs from the US and China next meet, a conversation that could shed light on when Beijing plans to resume purchases in earnest.

articles.title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Fading Rate-Cut Bets and Rising Oil
articles.content: Gold fell for a second day, after the release of US inflation data dimmed prospects for interest-rate cuts and the war in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher.

articles.title: Yen Nears Year’s Low as Strategists See Higher Intervention Bar
articles.content: The yen is hovering near its weakest level of the year against the dollar, while traders see a higher threshold for official intervention this time around.

articles.title: US to Release 172 Million Barrels of Oil for IEA Relief Plan
articles.content: The Trump administration plans to release 172 million barrels from the US emergency oil reserve as part of the coordinated effort by nations around the world to ease surging crude and fuel prices amid the Iran war.

articles.content: President Donald Trump’s administration started the first of several sweeping trade investigations that set the stage for new tariffs, the centerpiece of a push to replace levies struck down by the US Supreme Court.

articles.title: Private Credit Exodus Forces Caps at Cliffwater, Morgan Stanley
articles.content: Morgan Stanley and Cliffwater LLC capped withdrawals from their multibillion-dollar private credit funds after investors sought to redeem vastly more than the vehicles allow.

articles.title: Iran war: Trump will release 172 million barrels of oil from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
articles.content: The U.S. currently has 415 million barrels of oil in its strategic reserve, about 58% of the authorized capacity of 714 million barrels.

articles.title: Trump administration launches Section 301 trade probes into Mexico, China, EU, others
articles.content: The Supreme Court, in a 6-3 ruling, said President Trump was not authorized by the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, IEEPA, to impose tariffs.

articles.content: Google said its GFiber unit is combing with Astound Broadband and forming a independent fiber provider.

articles.title: IEA agrees to release record 400 million barrels of oil to address Iran war supply disruption
articles.content: The move comes as the Iran war continues to choke off traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: India-China reset? Relaxed rules allow Beijing to invest in India after about six years of friction
articles.content: The Indian cabinet has approved changes to its foreign direct investment policy, allowing investments from "Land Bordering Countries."

articles.title: Iran war: Trump says he's not worried about domestic terror attack
articles.content: President Donald Trump said oil companies should send tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, which has remained effectively closed due to the war on Iran.

articles.title: Atlassian slashes 10% of workforce to 'self-fund' investments in AI and enterprise sales
articles.content: Atlassian said it's cutting 10% of its workforce, or about 1,600 jobs, as it restructures to fund investments in artificial intelligence and enterprise sales.

articles.title: Oracle's TikTok stake sits at just over $2 billion, filing shows
articles.content: Oracle bought 15% of TikTok's U.S. operations in January after China's ByteDance was forced by U.S. law to divest.

articles.content: The Pentagon copied Tehran's technology but is still struggling to keep up.

articles.content: In Iran, Trump risks falling into a familiar trap.

articles.content: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since being chosen to succeed his father, fuelling speculation he was injured

articles.content: Kunal Shah, co-head of the US bank’s international business, made comments on call about the conflict

articles.content: Investors receive a few more updates related to the oil market Wednesday evening

articles.title: Why the IEA’s largest-ever release of oil reserves is pushing up crude prices
articles.content: The largest-ever release of oil from the International Energy Agency’s emergency stockpiles lifted prices for the commodity up by nearly 5% on Wednesday.

articles.title: Uber’s stock rises as new Amazon robotaxi partnership is ‘a positive surprise’
articles.content: Amazon’s Zoox still needs regulatory approval to use its custom-built robotaxis for commercial service

articles.title: Oracle’s stock surges. Here’s how it finally left the AI penalty box.
articles.content: Oracle is benefiting from its new bring-your-own-chip policy, which helps protect margins as the company wins more cloud business.

articles.title: ‘I didn’t ask a man to rear-end my car’: Social Security is replacing my disability benefits. Will the fund run out of money?
articles.content: “I didn’t ask a man to rear-end my car at a red light.”

articles.title: ‘I have lost nearly everything’: My mother’s trustee changed her $1 million will and my attorney fleeced me. What can I do?
articles.content: “My attorney, who described himself as a minister doing ‘God’s work,’ had me sign a 40% contingency agreement.”

articles.title: Petco is leaning on services, fresh food and pet tarantulas — and it is paying off
articles.content: Shares of Petco Health & Wellness jumped after hours on Wednesday after the pet-supplies retailer said it expects sales to rebound this year, as efforts to overhaul its store selections and lean more into services like dog grooming and training are starting to pay off.

articles.title: As Starbucks’ Howard Schultz heads to Miami, here’s a list of other billionaire business leaders moving to Florida
articles.content: A few Big Tech execs have also headed to the Sunshine State in recent months

articles.title: Iranian school was on U.S. target list, may have been mistaken as military site
articles.content: The strike on an Iranian elementary school killed at least 175, many of them children, raising questions as to whether the military’s use of AI-enabled targeting was a factor.

articles.title: Aid worker killed in drone strike on building used by Congo relief staff
articles.content: French aid worker Karine Buisset died in the attack. Two others were also killed, according to rebel group M23. Congo’s government and M23 blamed each other.

articles.title: Nations agree to release oil reserves as war in Iran hits global economy
articles.content: The International Energy Agency announced that it would carry out its largest-ever release of oil reserves — 400 million barrels.

articles.title: After Iran assault, Russians say U.S. can’t be trusted in Ukraine talks
articles.content: As Washington focuses on its push to topple Iran’s government, delaying talks on Russia’s war in Ukraine, some in Moscow say the Kremlin must achieve its goals militarily.

articles.title: As Israel targets Iran, Gaza’s nascent recovery stalls and Hamas gains strength
articles.content: Food deliveries and the prospect of medical care abroad gave Gazans reasons to hope, but the Iran conflict has closed the door on progress again.

articles.title: Qatar PM urges resilience and unity amid Iran strikes
articles.content: Qatar’s Prime Minister calls for strengthening the country’s ability to withstand “hardship” amid regional war.

articles.title: Iran’s sea mines raise fears of Strait of Hormuz oil disruption
articles.content: Tehran may seek to choke off oil traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz following US and Israeli strikes on Iran, raising fears it could deploy sea mines, officials say. President Donald Trump said US forces struck 28 Iranian mine-laying vessels as concerns grow over threats to the strategic shipping lane.

articles.title: US lawmakers probe Epstein’s wealth as ex-accountant testifies on finances and ties
articles.content: US House lawmakers on Wednesday examined Jeffrey Epstein’s financial empire as a committee questioned his former accountant in a closed-door deposition. Richard Kahn, an executor of Epstein’s estate, told lawmakers he had not seen evidence of abuse but described how the financier accumulated hundreds of millions and cultivated ties with powerful figures.

articles.title: Spain formally dismisses ambassador to Israel amid tensions with Tel Aviv
articles.content: Ambassador Sálomon had been recalled for consultations in September 2025 amid deteriorating relations between the two countries remained in Spain after that. Her dismissal now formalises the absence of a Spanish ambassador to Israel.

articles.title: Serbia and Albania issue joint text on EU accession without veto
articles.content: In a joint op-ed published in a German newspaper, the Serbian and Albanian leaders argued for a phased EU accession process that would involve waiving their right of veto.

articles.title: France's Macron opposed to lifting sanctions against Russia
articles.content: French President Emmanuel Macron also said he had "no confirmation" Iran was laying sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: EU reinforces cooperation with Azerbaijan on security and energy
articles.content: As the Iran war rages on, European Council President António Costa held talks with Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev in Baku on Wednesday to expand the European Union’s cooperation with Azerbaijan on defence, security and energy.

articles.title: Iran war: Is the EU a mere spectator or diplomatic player? MEPs debate on The Ring
articles.content: With the US and Israel’s war against Iran in its second week, the EU is bracing itself for a potential energy shock and economic fallout. As the conflict persists, can the EU continue to stand back and limit the damage, and is it facing a moment of reckoning as the guardian of the rules-based order?

articles.title: Michelin Guide 2026: The ten new Portuguese restaurants honoured with a star
articles.content: The good times continue to roll for Portuguese haute cuisine with ten new restaurants now boasting a Michelin star, while Fifty Seconds received its second afte a gala award night in Funchal.

articles.title: Hungary and Slovakia resist renewal of Russia sanctions as deadline nears
articles.content: The European Union wants to renew the individual sanctions on Russia by 15 March. But Hungary and Slovakia are blocking the decision. Tensions are running high between the three over the damaged Druzhba oil pipeline.

articles.title: Taking part in World Cup 'not possible', says Iranian sports minister
articles.content: The Iranian sports and youth minister Ahmad Donyamali told state television that his country's football players would not be safe in the US, which will be one of the World Cup's host countries this summer.

articles.title: US launches probe into trading partners including EU, China and India
articles.content: The move comes weeks after the US Supreme Court struck down a key part of Trump's tariffs policies.

articles.content: Morgan Stanley and Cliffwater LLC capped withdrawals from their multibillion-dollar private credit funds after investors sought to redeem vastly more than the vehicles allow.

articles.title: Oil Jumps as Tankers in Iraq Hit, IEA Fails to Ease Supply Fears
articles.content: Brent crude rallied back toward $100 a barrel after two tankers were attacked in Iraqi waters, underscoring the broader risks to energy assets across the Middle East and overshadowing a record oil release from the IEA.

articles.title: Two Tankers Attacked In Iraq Waters, Oil Terminals Suspended
articles.content: Two oil tankers have been attacked in Iraqi waters, according to the country’s state oil marketer, prompting the nation’s oil terminals to suspend operations.

articles.title: Japan Has Spent Decades Preparing For An Energy Crisis. Is It Enough?
articles.content: Japan’s dependence on overseas energy supplies — laid bare by an oil shock in 1973 — has shaped its policy for more than half a century.

articles.title: Hedge Funds Load Up on Aussie Call Options as RBA Decision Nears
articles.content: Hedge funds are ramping up bullish bets on the Australian dollar in the options markets before next week’s central bank decision.

articles.title: Stocks Drop, Crude Jumps as Energy Assets Targeted: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity futures and Asian stocks declined on Thursday, extending a volatile week, as another rally in oil prices and mounting strain in the private credit market weighed on investor sentiment.

articles.title: Wild Stock-Market Swings Make South Korean Options Look Cheap
articles.content: The South Korean stock market has been so turbulent in recent days that expectations for future swings pale in comparison.

articles.title: The next 7 days in Iran will determine if we face stagflation or a total global recession
articles.content: Your portfolio probably isn’t ready for the Iran conflict’s escalation.

articles.title: Trump’s Iran war tests US voters’ patience as petrol prices rise
articles.content: Administration has failed to douse surge in fuel costs as Middle East turmoil deepens

articles.title: Iran’s authorities warn against protests as Israel threatens Basij forces
articles.content: Police chief says protesters seen as acting at the behest of the US and Israel will be treated as enemies.

articles.title: Strikes between Israel and Hezbollah ramp up as evacuation notice issued for Beirut
articles.content: The Israel Defense Forces said Wednesday it began a "large-scale wave of strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure" in Beirut.

articles.title: ISIS-inspired teens considered other targets before Gracie Mansion protest: Sources
articles.content: The two teens charged with attempting to carry out what officials described as an ISIS-inspired attack in New York considered other targets before the mayor's residence.

articles.title: US launches probe into trading partners including the EU, China and India
articles.content: The move comes weeks after the US Supreme Court struck down a key part of Trump's tariffs policies.

articles.title: Lilly to Invest $3 Billion in China to Boost Obesity Pill
articles.content: Eli Lilly & Co will invest $3 billion in China over the next decade to expand local production, with a focus on weight loss as the US drugmaker looks to cement its dominance in the booming obesity market.

articles.title: Dow futures down more than 400 points as oil prices continue to climb
articles.content: Stock futures tumbled after hours on Wednesday, building on earlier losses for the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average, as crude-oil prices pressed higher.

articles.title: Trump Touts Oil Supply Moves, Vows to Finish in Iran
articles.content: President Donald Trump said the release of emergency oil reserves would ease energy price pressures while the US seeks to finish its campaign against Iran. Bloomberg's Rong Wei Neo has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Allianz, Sun Life Are Said to Weigh Bids for HSBC Life Singapore
articles.content: Allianz SE and Sun Life Financial Inc. are considering bids for HSBC Holdings Plc’s insurance unit in Singapore, according to people familiar with the matter, after the bank announced a strategic review of the business.

articles.title: Oil climbs back towards $100 after ships hit
articles.content: 400mn barrels of crude made available as governments race to tackle energy shock
